item.title: 'Tiger Balm' | Tee Exhibition | MMMRecords, Itaewon, Seoul, S.Korea
item.description: ‘Tiger Balm’ tee, part of a t-shirt exhibition at @mmmrecords Itaewon, Seoul. Huge thanks to @dekal for making all the tees and setting up the exhibition! absolute legend! The second part of the exhibition was intended to take place at @yesultong but unfortunately had to be cancelled due to covid restrictions, hopefully we can set something else up soon to take it’s place in the near future!

item.title: 'Sexy Beast' | Film 4 | "I know a bloke, who knows a bloke, who knows a bloke"
item.description: SexyBeast directed by JonathanGlazer, film4 Production Featuring Ray Winstone BenKingsley & IanMcShane. Film Poster illustration for a silkscreen print. Don: Yes! Gal: No! Don: Yes! Gal: No! Don: Fat cunt! Gal: No, No, No! Don: Yes, Yes, Yes!

item.title: 'Tiger Lair' Mural | Section Two | Gangnam, Seoul, S.Korea
item.description: Travel to a realm of enlightenment inspired by ancient Korean mythology. A tiger undergoes a spiritual journey shifting through layers of enlightenment on his path to becoming human. Along the way he encounters shrines, temples and mediation gardens inhabited by Buddha & Yama (The Lord of Hell).

item.title: 'Tiger Lair' Mural | Section One | Gangnam, Seoul, S.Korea
item.description: Travel to a realm of enlightenment inspired by ancient Korean mythology. A tiger undergoes a spiritual journey shifting through layers of enlightenment on his path to becoming human. Along the way he encounters shrines, temples and mediation gardens inhabited by Buddha & Yama (The Lord of Hell).

item.title: 'I K A R O A' | B&W & ULTRAVIOLET
item.description: I K A R O A - Framed, two colour silkscreen print. Taking its name from the Maori word for ‘the long fish that gave birth to all the stars in the Milky Way’ depicts a carp, bringing together Japanese and Maori culture within a retro framework.silkscreen print.

item.title: 'I K A R O A' | Illustration & Colour Variations
item.description: I K A R O A - Framed, two colour silkscreen print. Taking its name from the Maori word for ‘the long fish that gave birth to all the stars in the Milky Way’ depicts a carp, bringing together Japanese and Maori culture within a retro framework. silkscreen print.

item.title: 'Vishnu' | Framed Print
item.description: Vishnu is the Preserver and guardian of men (Narayana), he protects the order of things (dharma) and, when necessary, he appears on earth in various incarnations or avatars to fight demons and fierce creatures and so maintain cosmic harmony.

item.title: 'Punk Magpie' | 'Yelsultong Art Festival' Chungmuro, Seoul, S.Korea
item.description: I was standing on top of the scaffolding painting this magpie, I looked up and there sitting on the top of the wall directly above me is a magpie staring back at me, the little dude chilled with me for a while before heading off, a nice little seal of approval from the magpie massive!

item.title: 'Vishnu' | Illustration
item.description: Vishnu is the Preserver and guardian of men (Narayana), he protects the order of things (dharma) and, when necessary, he appears on earth in various incarnations or avatars to fight demons and fierce creatures and so maintain cosmic harmony.

item.title: '1842' | Pilsner Urquell
item.description: Sketch for a mural for Pilsner Urquell commission in homage to Alphonse Mucha. Hanganjin, Seoul, S.Korea.

item.title: 'I'am the Moon' | 'Decked Out' Exhibition Noksapyeong, Seoul
item.description: Skateboard for the ‘Decked Out’ Exhibition, Seoul, S.Korea

item.title: ‘The Short Analysis of Uncertainty’ | Exhibition, Daegu, S.Korea
item.description: Happy to be part of the upcoming ‘The Short Analysis of Uncertainty’ Exhibition, Daegu S.Korea - Twelve Artists, Twelve Days. Daegu Jung-gu Dalgubuldaero 2232-25

item.title: Collab w Animal Shelter | Seoul, S.Korea
item.description: Quality times working on a collab with @animal_shelter.

item.title: 'Monkey Terrorist' | Seoul
item.description: A piece looking at propaganda within media and mass consumption of false narratives. Here the monkey is representative of man. Unaware he’s reading his Daily Mail newspaper upside down, the monkey is a reflection into how the masses do not understand the content and underlying agendas they are fed.

item.title: Vans Apgujeong Grand Opening | Seoul, S.Korea
item.description: Illustration for Apgujeong Vans Store grand opening, Seoul, S.Korea.

item.title: 'House of Vans' | Event 2014 | Seoul, S.Korea
item.description: Illustration for ‘House Of Vans’ Event 2014. Thanks for the sweet shoes!

item.title: 'Kim Jong Un & Friends'
item.description: ‘Kim Jong Un & Friends’ - This collage piece was originally for a magazine commission I worked on which focused on the human rights issues in North Korea. A human rights commissioner was slandered by the North Korean regime for being gay, this piece play a twist on the story and hopefully sheds some light on the injustices happening within North Korea.
